title: Builder integration — settings into RuntimeBundle
purpose: Wire the settings loader and merger into build_runtime so that settings files populate the RuntimeBundle. Every value that was previously only settable via CLI flags or -c overrides can now come from a settings file, with CLI retaining highest precedence. This is the integration point between the config system (NC-001 through NC-003) and the runtime.
task: Modify build_runtime in norn-cli to load and merge settings before constructing the runtime. Populate AgentLoopConfig, ProviderConfigOverrides, and RetryPolicy from merged settings. Ensure CLI -c overrides take precedence over settings. Ensure profile reasoning_effort takes precedence over settings agent.reasoning_effort.

requirements.title: Load and merge settings in build_runtime
requirements.spec: WHEN build_runtime is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL load settings from all three file paths, merge them with five-layer precedence, and validate the result before proceeding with runtime construction.
acceptance: build_runtime calls norn::config::loader::load_settings early in its pipeline
Merged settings available before profile loading, CLI override application, and LoopContext construction
Settings loading errors propagate as BuildError::Argument
Test: build_runtime with a settings file picks up values from the file

requirements.title: AgentLoopConfig from settings
requirements.spec: WHEN merged settings contain agent section fields, THE SYSTEM SHALL populate the corresponding AgentLoopConfig fields. Duration strings SHALL be parsed via humantime.
acceptance: Test: settings agent fields flow into AgentLoopConfig

requirements.title: ProviderConfigOverrides from settings
requirements.spec: WHEN merged settings contain provider section fields, THE SYSTEM SHALL populate the corresponding ProviderConfigOverrides fields.
acceptance: Test: settings provider fields flow into ProviderConfigOverrides

requirements.title: RetryPolicy from settings
requirements.spec: WHEN merged settings contain retry section fields, THE SYSTEM SHALL construct a RetryPolicy from them.
acceptance: Settings retry values apply when no -c retry_* is set
Test: settings retry fields produce a RetryPolicy

requirements.title: CLI -c precedence over settings
requirements.spec: WHEN both a settings file and a -c key=value override set the same field, THE SYSTEM SHALL use the -c value. Settings provide defaults; CLI provides overrides.
acceptance: -c values are applied AFTER settings values in the pipeline
Test: -c override wins over settings value

requirements.title: Profile reasoning_effort precedence over settings
requirements.spec: WHEN both settings agent.reasoning_effort and a profile reasoning_effort are set, THE SYSTEM SHALL use the profile value. Profile is model-specific and should not be overridden by a global settings default.
acceptance: Settings agent.reasoning_effort=low + profile reasoning_effort=high produces high
Settings agent.reasoning_effort=low + no profile reasoning_effort produces low
No settings + profile reasoning_effort=medium produces medium

boundaries: SHALL NOT modify NornSettings struct — that was NC-002
SHALL NOT modify loader or merge logic — that was NC-003
SHALL NOT implement permission runtime evaluation — that is downstream
SHALL NOT implement hook registration from settings — that is the hooks cluster
SHALL NOT wire downstream config surfaces (skills, context) — that is NC-005
verification: cargo test -p norn -p norn-cli — all tests pass
cargo clippy -p norn -p norn-cli -- -D warnings clean
Settings values flow into AgentLoopConfig (verified by test with tempdir settings file)
CLI -c overrides take precedence over settings (verified by test)
Profile reasoning_effort overrides settings (verified by test)
All existing builder.rs tests pass unchanged
No file exceeds 500 lines of code (excluding tests, comments, whitespace)
